required_tools.reason: All tasks require GitHub repository operations — scaffolding the cto-pay repo (Task 1), committing Anchor program code (Tasks 2-4), test files (Tasks 5-6), audit reports (Task 7), and CI workflows.

Task 1 provisions cluster resources in the cto namespace: SeaweedFS bucket creation (K8s Job), ExternalSecret CRs for operator keypair and Helius API key, ConfigMap cto-pay-infra-endpoints, and Secret validation. Tasks 1002-1004 directly create and verify Kubernetes resources.

Security audit (Task 7) requires deep code analysis — pattern searching for unchecked arithmetic, init_if_needed usage, unsafe blocks, missing signer checks, and CPI ordering across the Anchor program source tree. Also useful for code review during implementation tasks.

recommended_tools.reason: Anchor 0.30.1, Solana Web3.js, and SPL Token library documentation lookups would accelerate implementation of program instructions (Tasks 2-4) and test helpers (Tasks 5-6). Useful for verifying correct API usage for Bankrun, anchor_spl CPI patterns, and PDA derivation.

Useful for generating consolidated codebase snapshots for the security audit (Task 7) — the auditor needs full program context in a single view to assess cross-instruction vulnerabilities, PDA collision risks, and state update ordering.

Task 1 creates GitOps manifests in infra/gitops/ that would be deployed via ArgoCD. While not strictly required (kubectl apply works), ArgoCD integration ensures the ExternalSecrets, ConfigMap, and SeaweedFS bucket Job are managed declaratively and stay in sync.
